Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouvussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-560 o Fax (916) 322-0886
4pri123,2004
Mary Lou Gusman-Davis
Data Processing Manager III
Department of Finance
915 L Skeet, 6ft Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. l-04-066
Dear Ms. Gusman-Davis:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post-
ernployment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'.Aci).1 Because your question
is general in nature we are providing informal assistance. The Commission does not act
as a finder of fact. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71.)
aUESTIONS
1. Do you have to watt 12 months before working on any state IT projects
performing oversight functions?
2. Can you pur:ue private anployment or employment at another state agency as a
consultant while still employed by your current state employer, the Department of
Finance?
3. Can you perform oversight functions as a contractor on projects on which you
previouslyperformed oversight functions as part of your state job?
CONCLUSIONS
The revolving door provisions of the Act do not prevent you from accepting
employment with a private employer, but do require you to wait l2months before
you make any appearimces or colnmunications with your former state employer or
employers under certain circumstances. This includes the Department ofFinance
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
I
ardi.'n


File No. I-04-066
Page No. 2
and any state administrative agerLcy that you work for or r€present inthe 12
months preceding your projected retirement date of Decemb er 2a04.
2- The issue of whether you must separate from state employment to work as a
consultant for a private employer or with another state agency is not governed by
the Act. You will need to discuss these issues with your crrrent employer
regarding its policy on this matter. The revolving doorprovisions do ,6t b"gi., to
apply to you until you officially terminate your employment with a state agency.
Please note however, the conflict-of-interest provisions in sections S7100-i7103
may apply.
3. The permanent ban will prevent you from advising a client who is not the State of
Califomia in anyproceeding in which you werepersonally and substantially
involved with while serving as a Department of Finance enrployee.
FACTS
You are currently employed at the Departrnent of Finance in the Technology
Oversight and Security Unit as a Data Processing Manager III. You are responsible for
providing independent oversight and performing projeclassessments based on the State
of California IT Project Oversight Framework ("Framework') published in February
2003- The Framework is required to be implemented in all state agencies. your
responsibility is to review project reports submitted by vendors, 
"ondrr"t in-depth project
assessments and ensure departrnent compliance with the Framework for yo,r assigned
departments.
All state deparknents are required to conduct project oversight functions on
Department of Finance approved projects. Another unit within the bepartrnent of Finance
makes the project approval decisions, you are at times asked for your input before the
decisions are made but do not have the final say. Projects classified as "irigh- criticality
require departrnents to hire the oversight consultant from the vendor comriunity. As part
of your job, you make recornmendations to departments on how to improve project
success and identifr project problem areas. You do not get involved in seleciing vendors
to perform project oversight that is the responsibility of the deparhnent.
Your classification is as a designated employee position requiring the filing of a
statement of economic interests (Form 700) on a yearly basis. You have the following
plans:
o Last day atwork June 30,2004;
o Run vacation time until December 2004 andretire;
o would like to perform project oversight functions for state projects
working as a contractor for a vendor beginning somewhere between June
30,2004 and December 2004.


File No. I-04-066
Page No. 3
You request advice as to whether there are any restrictions that you need to be
aware of before you accept a position performing project oversight for any state IT
projects.
ANALYSIS
State administrative officials 2 who leave state senrice are subject to two tSpes of
post-governmental employment restrictions under the Act:
A "one-year ban" prohibiting a state anployee from communicating with his or
her former agency to influence the agency's adminiskative or legislative action' (section
87a0Q; and a "permanent ban" barring a state employee from switching sides in any
specific proceeding on which the employee worked while in state service (sections
87400-8740s).
One-Yem Ban
Section 87406(dxl) states:
"No designated ernployee of a state administrative agency, any officer,
ernployee, or consultant of a state administrative agency who holds a
position which entails the making or participation in making, of decisions
which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest,
and no member of a state administrative agency, of a period of one year
after leaving office or employment, shall, for compensation, act as agent
or attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other petrson, by making any
formal or informal appearance, or by making any oral or written
communication, before any state administrative agency, or officer or
employee thereof, for which he or she worked or rq)resented during the 12
months before leaving office or employment, if the appearance or
communication is made for the pu{pose of influe,ncing administrative or
legislative action, or influencing any action or proceeding involving the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, Iicense,-grant,
' A "state administrative official" is defined in section 87400(b) as "every member, officer,
employee or consultant of a state adminishative agency who as part of his or her om.ld responsibilities
engages in anyjudicial, quasi-judicial or otherproceeding in other ttran a purely clerical, sedretarial or
ministerial c ap acity."
3 "fnfluenciag legislative or administrative action" includes influencing'"by any means, including
brrt_not limited to the provision or use of information, statistics, studies or analyies.;, ($ 
-82032.)
"Administrative action" is defined in section 820A2 as "the proposal, drafting, develojment, consideratiorq
amendment, enactrnent, or defeat by any state agency of any rule, regulatiorl or other action in any
ratemaking proceeding or any quasi-legislative proceeding . . . ." Section 82037 defines "legislative action,,
as "the drafting, intoduction, consideration, modification, enachnent or defeat of any bill, risolution,
amendment, report, nomination or other matter by the Legislature or by either house-or any committee,
subcommittee, joint or select committee thereo{ or by a member or employee of the Legisiature acting in
his official capacity. 'Legislative action' also means the action of the Governor in approiing or vetoing any
bilt."


File No. I-04-066
Page No. 4
or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property.,,
You are aDataProcessing Manager III at the Department of Finance, and you
have indicated that you are required to file statements of "coro*ic 
interests on a yearly
basis. Regulation 18746.1(a)(2)provides that the prohibitions in section 87406(dX1)
apply to, "[aJny employee of a state administrative agency who holds a position that is
designated or should be designated in the agency's conflict ofinterest cide.,, Therefore,
you are subject to the one-year ban.
The One Year Ban's Applicability in your Case
For purposes of the one-year ban, your former state administrative agency
ernployer is considered to be any state administrative agency for which you-worked or
represented during the 72 months immediatelybefore leaving state office or employment.
A "'state administrative age,ncy'means every state officg deparfinent, division,-buieau,
board and commission, but does not include the Legislature, the courts or any agency in
the judicial branch ofgovernment." ($ 87a00(a).) Accordingly, should youieave state
service upon expiration of your accrued vacation leavg the Deparknent of Finance will
be your former state administrative agency employer.
Your letter indicates that you are considering working as a consultant with
another state department while on vacation with the Department of Finance. Section
87406 was drafted to do the following:
"[P]rovide that no designated employee of a state administrative
agancy...for a period of one year after leaving office or employment, shall,
for compensation, make specified representation for any otherperson
before, among others, a state agency for which he or she worked or
rqrresented during the 12 months before leaving office or e,rnployment, if
the representation is made for the purpose of influencing adminirtrutir"
action." (Bill Analysis of Senate Bill l314 (Marks) Deparhnent of
Finance, February l, 1990.)
The statute expressly provides that the prohibition is imposed for a period of one year
after leaving employment, and applies to any state administrative agencyfor which the
former employee worked or represented drtingthe l}months befoie leaving office or
employment. Under the literal language of the statute, you are required to look back 12
months from the time you separate from state service to determine which agencies you
are prohibited from lobbying. Any agency you worked for or represented during that
period would be covered by the one-year prohibition running from your date of
separation. (P"rry Advice Letter, No. A-94-004.)
With regard to the one year requirement, you have indicated that you intend to
have your last day at work on June 30,2}04,but will use vacation time until December
2004, when you plan to retire. The Commission has determined that the one year term
begins when an ernployee has permanently Ieft employment and is no longer ieceiving


F,eNi#i;::!
compensation, including compensation for unused vacation time. (Regulation
r8746.1(b)(l), weilAdviceLetter,No.A-97-247.) Therefore,theoneyearbanwill
begin for you when you officially retire from the Department of Finance, as you indicated
most likely in December 2004. You are not subject to the one year ban while you are on
vacation from the Department of Finance.' However, since you will continue to be a
state administrative official, any state administrative agency that you work for or
represent will be considered to be your former employer. (Regulation 13746.10)(6).) In
addition, the conflict of interest laws of section 87103 will continue to apply to you.
Limitations While Employed at the Department of Finance
You have asked about beginning employment with either a private vendor or
another state agency while still employed with the Department of Finance, but while you
are using vacation time. State and local agencies are authorizedto adopt statements of
incompatible activities that prohibit their ernployees from engaging in any outside
ernployment, activities, or enterprises which are inconsistent or in conflict with their
duties as agency employees, or which me subject to control or approval by their state or
local agency employer. (Govemment Code $ 19990, 1126, et. seq.) This area of law is
outside the jurisdiction of the Commission and we offer no advice thereon. Yo
wish to check with the Department of Finance's counsel regarding these issuer.t -u'
You also inquired as to whether you could partner with a vendor to contribute to a
state department request for a proposal bid while still employed with the Departrnent of
Finance. We encourage you to consider whether Government Code section 1090, the law
prohibiting public officials from having an interest in certain contracts within their own
agencies, may also be implicated by your situation. The Commission is charged with
interpreting and enforcing the provisions of the Act, and may provide advice only with
respect to those provisions ($ 831 l4), so we refer you to the Attorney General's office for
questions regarding Government Code section 1090.
Application to the Department of Finance After Termination of Employment
The one-year ban prohibits appearances and communications with your former
agency employer. "An appearance or cofilmunication includes, but is not limited to,
conversing by telephone or in person, corresponding with in writing or by electronic
transmission, attending a meeting, and delivering or sending any communication."
(Regulation ft7a6.2(a).) However, not all communications to a former state
administrative agency employer are prohibited by the one-yearban; it is only when the
communication is for the purpose of "influencing" that the communication implicates the
one-year ban. An appearance or communication "is for the purpose of influencing if it is
made for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing, moditdng,
a Contrast, however, leaves ofabsence. For purposes ofthe one-year ban, an official has left his or
her state employment when he or she has either permanently left state service or is on leave of absence.
(Regulati_on I 8746. 1 (bX 1 ).)
'Note tfrat as long as you are a "public official" the conflict of interest rules in sections 87100 and
87103 apply to you as well. We have enclose d a fact sheet for your information entitled: "Can I Vote?
Overview of the Conflicts Laws." The fact sheet explains the conflict-of-interest analysis.


File No. I-04-066
Page No. 6
opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding." (Regulation lS7a6.2(a).)
You should be aware, however, that the one-year ban does not apply to services
performed "to administer, implement, or fulfill the requirements of an oirii"g permit,
license, 9ffi1, contract, or sale agreement... provided the services do not involve the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of any of these actions or proceedings.,,
(Regulation 18746.1O)(5XA); Hanan Advice Letter, No. I-0G209; BtlleiiAdviceLetter,
No. I-00-234.) Thus, the one-year ban does not apply if you were to be retained by
another person to provide consulting services that involve you exclusively in
administering, implementing, or fulfilling the requirements of an existing contract to
which the Department of Finance is a party.
In addition, if you leave the Departrnent of Finance and work for a private
employer, you could not be identified in communications to your former employer for
one year- You also could not personally contact or meet with any official oiemployee of
the Departrnent of Finance conceming anyprojects. (MarovichAdvice Letter, No. A-99-
l3l; Bohart Advice Letter, No. I-99-231.) On the otherhand, the one-yearprohibition
would not prohibit you from drafting documents on behalf ofan €rnployer to be
submitted to the Department of Finance as long as you arenot identified in connection
with the communicalion. Moreover, you could use your expertise to advise a private
employer or client about Deparhnent of Financeprocedureq Framework pro""d*"*, etc.,
as long as you are not identified with the employer or client's efforts to influence
Deparhnent of Finance action on a contract.
The facts state that you intend to work for a vendor as a contractor to perform
project oversight functions for state projects. As a state employee you review pdect
reports submitted by vendors to ensure department compliance. Therefore, you would be
banned for one year from communicating with the Deparhnent of Finan"" o1any of these
areas. As for performing oversight on any state IT projects with a private vendor, you
may do this within one year, as long as you do not make any appearances or
communications with the Department of Finance.
Application to Other State Administrative Agencies after Termination of
Employment
The one-year ban would not prohibit you from providing consulting services that
involve your appearance before or communication with other state adminis-hative
agencies, unless the agency is subject to the direction and conhol of the Departnent of
Finance or it is one that you worked for or represented while you were on iacation
through Decernber 2A04. (Regulation I 87a6. I (b)(6).)
The Permanent Ban
Sections 87401 and 87402 prohibit a former state adminishative official from
advising or representing any person, other than the State of California, for compensation


FileNo. I-04-066
Page No. 7
in anyjudicial, quasi-judicial or otherproceeding in which the official participated while
in state service. Specifically, section 87401provides:
"No former state administrative official, after the termination of his or her
employment or term of office, shall for compensation act as agent or
attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other person (other than the State
of california) before any court or state adminiskative agency or other
officer or employee thereofby making any formal or informal appearance,
or by making any oral or written communication with the intent to
influence, in connection with anyjudicial, quasi-judiciar or other
proceeding if both of the following apply:
(a) The State of California is a pmty or has a direct and zubstantial interest-
(b) The proceeding is one in which the former state administrative official
participated."
In addition, under section 87402 a former state administrative official shall not,
for compensation, "aid, advise, counsel, consult or assist in representing any other person
(except the State of California) in anyproceeding in which thi official *ouia U"
prohibited from appearing under Section 87401.- Significantly, unlike the one-yearban,
the permanent ban is not restricted to proceedings before a former official's prior state
administrative employer. The permanent ban also does not apply to "new,, pioceedings,
including new contracts in which the former employee did not farticipate. iS gZ+Of ;-
Regulation 18741.1(a)(5).) Again, as a Data Processing Manager III who isiequired to
file statements of economic interests on a yearly basis, you are a state administrative
official and subject to the permanent ban. ($ S7400(b).)
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceeding in which you participated while a state administrative ofnciat at the
Deparhnent of Finance or other state agency. "'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other
proceeding' means any proceeding application, request for a ruling or other
determination, contract, claim, controversy, investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or
other particular matter involving a specific party or parties in any court or state
administrative agency...." ($ 87a00(c).) It includes a proceeding in which state
administrative officials participate, but leave state employ before the proceeding
concludes.
An of,ficial is considered to have "participated" in a proceeding if the official was
personally and substantially involved in the proceeding. ($ 87400(d).i A former state
official who held a management position in a state administrative agency is deemed to
have participated in a proceeding if : (1) the proceeding was pending beiore the agency
during his or her tenure, and Q) the proceeding was under his or heisupervisory
authority. ($ 87400(d); Regulation t874r.r({(a).) To apply the permanent ban to your
situation, you will need to identifr the proceedings in wtriitr you purti"ipated while
employed by the state.


File No. I-04-066
Page No. 8
"Supervisory authority" is not a phrase defined under the Act. This phrase was
adopted by the commission in the BrownAdvice Letter,No. A-91-033.6 Therg an
official was advised that he was deemed to have personally and substantially participated
in all proceedings of his former agency, if those proceedings were in his chain of
command during his tenure at the agency. In In re Lucas (2000) 14 FppC Ops. 15, the
Commission clarified that not all proceedings subordinate to an official within his or her
chain of command are considered 'hnder his or her supervisory authoity.,, (Regulation
18741-l(a)(a)-) The Commission concluded there that an official's general administrative
oversight of a program to be carried out by those subordinate to the official on an
agency's organizational chart was insufiEcient to rise to the level of 'lersonal and
substantial" involvement required by the Act. Thus, generally a procleding is under an
official's supervisory authority when the proceeding ii not only subordinatl to the official
under the agency's organizational structurg but when the official also undertakes one or
more of the activities described in section g7400(d).
Restrictions on Projects on which you performed overcight
The permanent ban will prohibit you from advising orrepresenting anyperson,
other than the State of Califomia for compensation in p.o""edirrgr in whicl youhave
participated while in state service. Without additional facts, the-Commission cannot
determine in which proceedings you participated or w€re personally and substantially
involved while in state service. Please note again the special situation discussed above
for individuals employed as supervisors. If the permanent ban may apply to you, you
should seek further assistance.
If you have any other questions regarding this mattetr, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Rector
Intem, Legal Division

6In Brown,the Commission considered a request from a fomrer chief of its Enforcement Division,
concerning applicability of the pemranent ban to his post-retirement representation in an enforcement
proceeding that commenced in the waning weeks ofhis state service. The Commission rejected his
argument that he was not personally and substantially involved in that proceeding since nL substantive
work was undertaken either by himself or enforcement staff in the matier during his tenure.

The revolving door provisions of the Act do not prevent you from accepting
employment with a private employer, but do require you to wait l2months before

employers under certain circumstances. This includes the Department ofFinance

and any state administrative agerLcy that you work for or r€present inthe 12

sections.facts:
You are currently employed at the Departrnent of Finance in the Technology
Oversight and Security Unit as a Data Processing Manager III. You are responsible for
providing independent oversight and performing projeclassessments based on the State
of California IT Project Oversight Framework ("Framework') published in February
2003- The Framework is required to be implemented in all state agencies. your
responsibility is to review project reports submitted by vendors, 
"ondrr"t in-depth project
assessments and ensure departrnent compliance with the Framework for yo,r assigned
departments.
All state deparknents are required to conduct project oversight functions on
Department of Finance approved projects. Another unit within the bepartrnent of Finance
makes the project approval decisions, you are at times asked for your input before the
decisions are made but do not have the final say. Projects classified as "irigh- criticality
require departrnents to hire the oversight consultant from the vendor comriunity. As part
of your job, you make recornmendations to departments on how to improve project
success and identifr project problem areas. You do not get involved in seleciing vendors
to perform project oversight that is the responsibility of the deparhnent.
Your classification is as a designated employee position requiring the filing of a
statement of economic interests (Form 700) on a yearly basis. You have the following
plans:
o Last day atwork June 30,2004;
o Run vacation time until December 2004 andretire;
o would like to perform project oversight functions for state projects
working as a contractor for a vendor beginning somewhere between June
30,2004 and December 2004.
You request advice as to whether there are any restrictions that you need to be
aware of before you accept a position performing project oversight for any state IT
projects.
sections.analysis:
State administrative officials 2 who leave state senrice are subject to two tSpes of
post-governmental employment restrictions under the Act:
A "one-year ban" prohibiting a state anployee from communicating with his or
her former agency to influence the agency's adminiskative or legislative action' (section
87a0Q; and a "permanent ban" barring a state employee from switching sides in any
specific proceeding on which the employee worked while in state service (sections
87400-8740s).
One-Yem Ban
Section 87406(dxl) states:
"No designated ernployee of a state administrative agency, any officer,
ernployee, or consultant of a state administrative agency who holds a
position which entails the making or participation in making, of decisions
which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest,
and no member of a state administrative agency, of a period of one year
after leaving office or employment, shall, for compensation, act as agent
or attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other petrson, by making any
formal or informal appearance, or by making any oral or written
communication, before any state administrative agency, or officer or
employee thereof, for which he or she worked or rq)resented during the 12
months before leaving office or employment, if the appearance or
communication is made for the pu{pose of influe,ncing administrative or
legislative action, or influencing any action or proceeding involving the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, Iicense,-grant,
' A "state administrative official" is defined in section 87400(b) as "every member, officer,
employee or consultant of a state adminishative agency who as part of his or her om.ld responsibilities
engages in anyjudicial, quasi-judicial or otherproceeding in other ttran a purely clerical, sedretarial or
ministerial c ap acity."
3 "fnfluenciag legislative or administrative action" includes influencing'"by any means, including
brrt_not limited to the provision or use of information, statistics, studies or analyies.;, ($ 
-82032.)
"Administrative action" is defined in section 820A2 as "the proposal, drafting, develojment, consideratiorq
amendment, enactrnent, or defeat by any state agency of any rule, regulatiorl or other action in any
ratemaking proceeding or any quasi-legislative proceeding . . . ." Section 82037 defines "legislative action,,
as "the drafting, intoduction, consideration, modification, enachnent or defeat of any bill, risolution,
amendment, report, nomination or other matter by the Legislature or by either house-or any committee,
subcommittee, joint or select committee thereo{ or by a member or employee of the Legisiature acting in
his official capacity. 'Legislative action' also means the action of the Governor in approiing or vetoing any
bilt."
or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property.,,
You are aDataProcessing Manager III at the Department of Finance, and you
have indicated that you are required to file statements of "coro*ic 
interests on a yearly
basis. Regulation 18746.1(a)(2)provides that the prohibitions in section 87406(dX1)
apply to, "[aJny employee of a state administrative agency who holds a position that is
designated or should be designated in the agency's conflict ofinterest cide.,, Therefore,
you are subject to the one-year ban.
The One Year Ban's Applicability in your Case
For purposes of the one-year ban, your former state administrative agency
ernployer is considered to be any state administrative agency for which you-worked or
represented during the 72 months immediatelybefore leaving state office or employment.
A "'state administrative age,ncy'means every state officg deparfinent, division,-buieau,
board and commission, but does not include the Legislature, the courts or any agency in
the judicial branch ofgovernment." ($ 87a00(a).) Accordingly, should youieave state
service upon expiration of your accrued vacation leavg the Deparknent of Finance will
be your former state administrative agency employer.
Your letter indicates that you are considering working as a consultant with
another state department while on vacation with the Department of Finance. Section
87406 was drafted to do the following:
"[P]rovide that no designated employee of a state administrative
agancy...for a period of one year after leaving office or employment, shall,
for compensation, make specified representation for any otherperson
before, among others, a state agency for which he or she worked or
rqrresented during the 12 months before leaving office or e,rnployment, if
the representation is made for the purpose of influencing adminirtrutir"
action." (Bill Analysis of Senate Bill l314 (Marks) Deparhnent of
Finance, February l, 1990.)
The statute expressly provides that the prohibition is imposed for a period of one year
after leaving employment, and applies to any state administrative agencyfor which the
former employee worked or represented drtingthe l}months befoie leaving office or
employment. Under the literal language of the statute, you are required to look back 12
months from the time you separate from state service to determine which agencies you
are prohibited from lobbying. Any agency you worked for or represented during that
period would be covered by the one-year prohibition running from your date of
separation. (P"rry Advice Letter, No. A-94-004.)
With regard to the one year requirement, you have indicated that you intend to
have your last day at work on June 30,2}04,but will use vacation time until December
2004, when you plan to retire. The Commission has determined that the one year term
begins when an ernployee has permanently Ieft employment and is no longer ieceiving


F,eNi#i;::!
compensation, including compensation for unused vacation time. (Regulation
r8746.1(b)(l), weilAdviceLetter,No.A-97-247.) Therefore,theoneyearbanwill
begin for you when you officially retire from the Department of Finance, as you indicated
most likely in December 2004. You are not subject to the one year ban while you are on
vacation from the Department of Finance.' However, since you will continue to be a
state administrative official, any state administrative agency that you work for or
represent will be considered to be your former employer. (Regulation 13746.10)(6).) In
addition, the conflict of interest laws of section 87103 will continue to apply to you.
Limitations While Employed at the Department of Finance
You have asked about beginning employment with either a private vendor or
another state agency while still employed with the Department of Finance, but while you
are using vacation time. State and local agencies are authorizedto adopt statements of
incompatible activities that prohibit their ernployees from engaging in any outside
ernployment, activities, or enterprises which are inconsistent or in conflict with their
duties as agency employees, or which me subject to control or approval by their state or
local agency employer. (Govemment Code $ 19990, 1126, et. seq.) This area of law is
outside the jurisdiction of the Commission and we offer no advice thereon. Yo
wish to check with the Department of Finance's counsel regarding these issuer.t -u'
You also inquired as to whether you could partner with a vendor to contribute to a
state department request for a proposal bid while still employed with the Departrnent of
Finance. We encourage you to consider whether Government Code section 1090, the law
prohibiting public officials from having an interest in certain contracts within their own
agencies, may also be implicated by your situation. The Commission is charged with
interpreting and enforcing the provisions of the Act, and may provide advice only with
respect to those provisions ($ 831 l4), so we refer you to the Attorney General's office for
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; CONCLUSION appears before QUESTION

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: s regarding Government Code section 1090.
Application to the Department of Finance After Termination of Employment
The one-year ban prohibits appearances and communications with your former
agency employer. "An appearance or cofilmunication includes, but is not limited to,
conversing by telephone or in person, corresponding with in writing or by electronic
transmission, attending a meeting, and delivering or sending any communication."
(Regulation ft7a6.2(a).) However, not all communications to a former state
administrative agency employer are prohibited by the one-yearban; it is only when the
communication is for the purpose of "influencing" that the communication implicates the
one-year ban. An appearance or communication "is for the purpose of influencing if it is
made for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing, moditdng,
a Contrast, however, leaves ofabsence. For purposes ofthe one-year ban, an official has left his or
her state employment when he or she has either permanently left state service or is on leave of absence.
(Regulati_on I 8746. 1 (bX 1 ).)
'Note tfrat as long as you are a "public official" the conflict of interest rules in sections 87100 and
87103 apply to you as well. We have enclose d a fact sheet for your information entitled: "Can I Vote?
Overview of the Conflicts Laws." The fact sheet explains the conflict-of-interest analysis.
opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding." (Regulation lS7a6.2(a).)
You should be aware, however, that the one-year ban does not apply to services
performed "to administer, implement, or fulfill the requirements of an oirii"g permit,
license, 9ffi1, contract, or sale agreement... provided the services do not involve the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of any of these actions or proceedings.,,
(Regulation 18746.1O)(5XA); Hanan Advice Letter, No. I-0G209; BtlleiiAdviceLetter,
No. I-00-234.) Thus, the one-year ban does not apply if you were to be retained by
another person to provide consulting services that involve you exclusively in
administering, implementing, or fulfilling the requirements of an existing contract to
which the Department of Finance is a party.
In addition, if you leave the Departrnent of Finance and work for a private
employer, you could not be identified in communications to your former employer for
one year- You also could not personally contact or meet with any official oiemployee of
the Departrnent of Finance conceming anyprojects. (MarovichAdvice Letter, No. A-99-
l3l; Bohart Advice Letter, No. I-99-231.) On the otherhand, the one-yearprohibition
would not prohibit you from drafting documents on behalf ofan €rnployer to be
submitted to the Department of Finance as long as you arenot identified in connection
with the communicalion. Moreover, you could use your expertise to advise a private
employer or client about Deparhnent of Financeprocedureq Framework pro""d*"*, etc.,
as long as you are not identified with the employer or client's efforts to influence
Deparhnent of Finance action on a contract.
The facts state that you intend to work for a vendor as a contractor to perform
project oversight functions for state projects. As a state employee you review pdect
reports submitted by vendors to ensure department compliance. Therefore, you would be
banned for one year from communicating with the Deparhnent of Finan"" o1any of these
areas. As for performing oversight on any state IT projects with a private vendor, you
may do this within one year, as long as you do not make any appearances or
communications with the Department of Finance.
Application to Other State Administrative Agencies after Termination of
Employment
The one-year ban would not prohibit you from providing consulting services that
involve your appearance before or communication with other state adminis-hative
agencies, unless the agency is subject to the direction and conhol of the Departnent of
Finance or it is one that you worked for or represented while you were on iacation
through Decernber 2A04. (Regulation I 87a6. I (b)(6).)
The Permanent Ban
Sections 87401 and 87402 prohibit a former state adminishative official from
advising or representing any person, other than the State of California, for compensation


FileNo. I-04-066
in anyjudicial, quasi-judicial or otherproceeding in which the official participated while
in state service. Specifically, section 87401provides:
"No former state administrative official, after the termination of his or her
employment or term of office, shall for compensation act as agent or
attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other person (other than the State
of california) before any court or state adminiskative agency or other
officer or employee thereofby making any formal or informal appearance,
or by making any oral or written communication with the intent to
influence, in connection with anyjudicial, quasi-judiciar or other
proceeding if both of the following apply:
(a) The State of California is a pmty or has a direct and zubstantial interest-
(b) The proceeding is one in which the former state administrative official
participated."
In addition, under section 87402 a former state administrative official shall not,
for compensation, "aid, advise, counsel, consult or assist in representing any other person
(except the State of California) in anyproceeding in which thi official *ouia U"
prohibited from appearing under Section 87401.- Significantly, unlike the one-yearban,
the permanent ban is not restricted to proceedings before a former official's prior state
administrative employer. The permanent ban also does not apply to "new,, pioceedings,
including new contracts in which the former employee did not farticipate. iS gZ+Of ;-
Regulation 18741.1(a)(5).) Again, as a Data Processing Manager III who isiequired to
file statements of economic interests on a yearly basis, you are a state administrative
official and subject to the permanent ban. ($ S7400(b).)
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceeding in which you participated while a state administrative ofnciat at the
Deparhnent of Finance or other state agency. "'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other
proceeding' means any proceeding application, request for a ruling or other
determination, contract, claim, controversy, investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or
other particular matter involving a specific party or parties in any court or state
administrative agency...." ($ 87a00(c).) It includes a proceeding in which state
administrative officials participate, but leave state employ before the proceeding
concludes.
An of,ficial is considered to have "participated" in a proceeding if the official was
personally and substantially involved in the proceeding. ($ 87400(d).i A former state
official who held a management position in a state administrative agency is deemed to
have participated in a proceeding if : (1) the proceeding was pending beiore the agency
during his or her tenure, and Q) the proceeding was under his or heisupervisory
authority. ($ 87400(d); Regulation t874r.r({(a).) To apply the permanent ban to your
situation, you will need to identifr the proceedings in wtriitr you purti"ipated while
employed by the state.
"Supervisory authority" is not a phrase defined under the Act. This phrase was
adopted by the commission in the BrownAdvice Letter,No. A-91-033.6 Therg an
official was advised that he was deemed to have personally and substantially participated
in all proceedings of his former agency, if those proceedings were in his chain of
command during his tenure at the agency. In In re Lucas (2000) 14 FppC Ops. 15, the
Commission clarified that not all proceedings subordinate to an official within his or her
chain of command are considered 'hnder his or her supervisory authoity.,, (Regulation
18741-l(a)(a)-) The Commission concluded there that an official's general administrative
oversight of a program to be carried out by those subordinate to the official on an
agency's organizational chart was insufiEcient to rise to the level of 'lersonal and
substantial" involvement required by the Act. Thus, generally a procleding is under an
official's supervisory authority when the proceeding ii not only subordinatl to the official
under the agency's organizational structurg but when the official also undertakes one or
more of the activities described in section g7400(d).
Restrictions on Projects on which you performed overcight
The permanent ban will prohibit you from advising orrepresenting anyperson,
other than the State of Califomia for compensation in p.o""edirrgr in whicl youhave
participated while in state service. Without additional facts, the-Commission cannot
determine in which proceedings you participated or w€re personally and substantially
involved while in state service. Please note again the special situation discussed above
for individuals employed as supervisors. If the permanent ban may apply to you, you
should seek further assistance.

CONCLUSION: The revolving door provisions of the Act do not prevent you from accepting
employment with a private employer, but do require you to wait l2months before
you make any appearimces or colnmunications with your former state employer or
employers under certain circumstances. This includes the Department ofFinance
I
ardi.'n
and any state administrative agerLcy that you work for or r€present inthe 12
months preceding your projected retirement date of Decemb er 2a04.
2- The issue of whether you must separate from state employment to work as a
consultant for a private employer or with another state agency is not governed by
the Act. You will need to discuss these issues with your crrrent employer
regarding its policy on this matter. The revolving doorprovisions do ,6t b"gi., to
apply to you until you officially terminate your employment with a state agency.
Please note however, the conflict-of-interest provisions in sections S7100-i7103
may apply.
3. The permanent ban will prevent you from advising a client who is not the State of
Califomia in anyproceeding in which you werepersonally and substantially
involved with while serving as a Department of Finance enrployee.

The Commission does not act as a finder of fact. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71.) aUESTIONS 1. Do you have to watt 12 months before working on any state IT projects performing oversight functions? 2. Can you pur:ue private anployment or employment at another state agency as a consultant while still employed by your current state employer, the Department of Finance? 3. Can you perform oversight functions as a contractor on projects on which you previouslyperformed oversight functions as part of your state job? CONCLUSIONS The revolving door provisions of the Act do not prevent you from accepting employment with a private employer, but do require you to wait l2months before you make any appearimces or colnmunications with your former state employer or employers under certain circumstances. This includes the Department ofFinance I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations. I ardi.'n File No. I-04-066 Page No. 2 and any state administrative agerLcy that you work for or r€present inthe 12 months preceding your projected retirement date of Decemb er 2a04. 2- The issue of whether you must separate from state employment to work as a consultant for a private employer or with another state agency is not governed by the Act. You will need to discuss these issues with your crrrent employer regarding its policy on this matter. The revolving doorprovisions do ,6t b"gi., to apply to you until you officially terminate your employment with a state agency. Please note however, the conflict-of-interest provisions in sections S7100-i7103 may apply. 3. The permanent ban will prevent you from advising a client who is not the State of Califomia in anyproceeding in which you werepersonally and substantially involved with while serving as a Department of Finance enrployee. FACTS You are currently employed at the Departrnent of Finance in the Technology Oversight and Security Unit as a Data Processing Manager III. You are responsible for providing independent oversight and performing projeclassessments based on the State of California IT Project Oversight Framework ("Framework') published in February 2003- The Framework is required to be implemented in all state agencies. your responsibility is to review project reports submitted by vendors, "ondrr"t in-depth project assessments and ensure departrnent compliance with the Framework for yo,r assigned departments. All state deparknents are required to conduct project oversight functions on Department of Finance approved projects. Another unit within the bepartrnent of Finance makes the project approval decisions, you
